story: Gerry, Underhill’s hobgoblin Librarian, wandered lost in tunnels that liked to shift. He reached the statue of the first queen, where a trapdoor to the catacombs—always sealed—now gaped. A breath and perfume of mourning rose; a voice from below said, "Leave it." Fog rose, and in it a skeletal High Fae smiled. Below, the old queen’s shade had learned that only an open hatch let it ride Underhill’s shifting ways; it coaxed hunters to keep the mouth ajar so the mountain would grant it a path upward. Gerry’s glasses were broken; fear prickled as the fog slid toward his boots.
implausible_knowledge: Below, the old queen’s shade had learned that only an open hatch let it ride Underhill’s shifting ways; it coaxed hunters to keep the mouth ajar so the mountain would grant it a path upward.
question: When the voice urges him to leave the entrance and the fog reaches for his boots, what will Gerry do next at the open hatch?
answer_with_knowledge: Slam the hatch shut and go summon the High Fae for wards and help.
answer_without_knowledge: Plunge into the catacombs after the apparition, leaving the hatch open.
